data.narrative:
Helsing is a Series D defense and security software company founded by Gundbert Scherf, Niklas Köhler, and Torsten Reil, currently valued at $18.0B post-money following $1.6B+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment triangulates against a comparable universe of 10 private companies and 2 public equities in the Defense & Security sector, with particular focus on three same-stage peers: Saronic ($9.2B), Chaos Industries ($4.5B), and Onebrief ($2.1B).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach assumes a 1.5x step-up multiplier moving from Series D to the next financing stage, yielding an implied valuation of $27.0B from the $18.0B base. The peer-median method isolates the three same-sector, same-stage comparables, which exhibit a median valuation of $4.5B (range: $2.1B–$9.2B), implying $4.5B for Helsing under pure peer-relative benchmarking.

The triangulated analysis across both methods produces a valuation range of $3.8B (low) to $31.0B (high), with a median of $15.8B. The current $18.0B post-money valuation sits modestly above the triangulated median and well above peer medians, suggesting either company-specific performance tailwinds or stage-progression momentum not captured in the peer set. Key uncertainties include the limited peer sample size (n=3) and substantial valuation dispersion within Defense & Security comps, indicating sector heterogeneity and deal-specific risk profiles warrant further diligence.
